nty9fq7.text: This is one of those tools that feels harmless until you actually use it and immediately regret it. Seeing subscriptions as a list is fine. Seeing them as big angry boxes competing for space in your budget hits different. Also respect for no signup and local-only data. Anything that touches money instantly gets more trust when it doesn’t ask for an email first. Out of curiosity, did you consider adding a “hide this one because I’m not ready emotionally” toggle? Asking for a friend.

ntsoha5.text: This grid is a game changer, finally a way to visualize how much I'm really bleeding every month.

ntu3s9w.text: i have not subscribed to anything.

ntsuu2o.text: not doubting you, but wtf is the relationship to tree's in these? :D I get that you would have the large block with the next largest next to it, which is tree'ish I guess.

ntu5xa0.text: Full tree maps are nested. So the most common usecase in computing is a program like WinDirStat that essentially shows you which folders are taking up the most space on a drive. So you take your tree C:\ 129 GB ├─ Windows 39 GB │ ├─ Drivers 15 GB │ ├─ DLLs 10 GB │ └─ Bins 14 GB │ └─ Program Files 80 GB ├─ Photoshop 40 GB ├─ Chrome 1 GB └─ Steam 39 GB Which as a tree map looks a bit like this C:\ 129 GB ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ │ WINDOWS 39 GB │ PROGRAM FILES 80 GB │ │ │ │ │ ┌────────────────┐ │ ┌────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Drivers 15 GB │ │ │ Photoshop 40 GB │ │ │ └────────────────┘ │ └────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ┌──────────────┐ │ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ │ │ │ DLLs 10 GB │ │ │ Chrome │ │ Steam 39 GB │ │ │ └──────────────┘ │ │ 1 GB │ └──────────────────┘ │ │ ┌────────────────┐ │ └──────────┘ │ │ │ Bins 14 GB │ │ │ │ └────────────────┘ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

ntu60z6.text: Copy pasting because this was too much effort to only show once: Full tree maps are nested. So the most common usecase in computing is a program like WinDirStat that essentially shows you which folders are taking up the most space on a drive. So you take your tree C:\ 129 GB ├─ Windows 39 GB │ ├─ Drivers 15 GB │ ├─ DLLs 10 GB │ └─ Bins 14 GB │ └─ Program Files 80 GB ├─ Photoshop 40 GB ├─ Chrome 1 GB └─ Steam 39 GB Which as a tree map looks a bit like this C:\ 129 GB ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ │ WINDOWS 39 GB │ PROGRAM FILES 80 GB │ │ │ │ │ ┌────────────────┐ │ ┌────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Drivers 15 GB │ │ │ Photoshop 40 GB │ │ │ └────────────────┘ │ └────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ┌──────────────┐ │ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ │ │ │ DLLs 10 GB │ │ │ Chrome │ │ Steam 39 GB │ │ │ └──────────────┘ │ │ 1 GB │ └──────────────────┘ │ │ ┌────────────────┐ │ └──────────┘ │ │ │ Bins 14 GB │ │ │ │ └────────────────┘ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
